claim_status: Native UI readiness-review template only; no owner review, no independent review, no ADR-0013 approval, no ADR-0014 approval, no ADR-0016 approval, no UI-GATE-7 claim, no UI-GATE-10 claim, no native adapter prototype claim, no UI toolkit selection, no toolkit selection, no trusted-chrome readiness claim, no accessibility readiness claim, no screen-reader coverage claim, no page-tree composition proof, no page-surface approval, no compositor ownership decision, no release-path UI approval, no production claim, and no implementation claim.

review_scope.prohibited_placeholder_policy: Template records must not name placeholder reviewers, title-only approval, self-approval, selected toolkit placeholders, reference-platform placeholders, or implied owner authority; a real readiness review must replace null fields with owner-reviewed evidence.

gate_axes.axis: Toolkit-neutral UI model and adapter contract
gate_axes.required_evidence: Accepted ADR-0013, complete toolkit-neutral state, command, surface, accessibility, diagnostic, and adapter contracts, native adapter prototype evidence, no toolkit-owned authority negative tests, and owner review beyond the checked no-claim native UI readiness-review template.

Equivalent Slint, Vizia, and Floem or GPUI reference-shell adapters or owner-approved reduced scope, accepted ADR-0014, accessibility, IME, keyboard, crash, GPU-loss, startup, memory, binary, latency, frame-pacing, energy, license, dependency, provenance, replacement, package, runtime-exclusion, and owner review beyond the checked no-claim native UI readiness-review template.

Executable UI-GATE-7 page-surface prototype, accepted ADR-0016, typed page-surface handles, brokered surface handle proof, document and device generations, resize, scale, damage, input, IME, accessibility, occlusion, capture, renderer crash, GPU-loss, software fallback, stale-handle negative tests, latency, frame-pacing, compositor ownership decision, and owner review beyond the checked no-claim native UI readiness-review template.

gate_axes.axis: Design tokens, component inventory, and fixtures
gate_axes.required_evidence: Rendered design-token and component-fixture pack, adapter-specific browser chrome, tabs, Spaces, command field, permission prompts, agent confirmations, resource manager, settings, recovery UI, keyboard, focus, screen-reader, forced-color, high contrast, reduced motion, density, localization, error-state outputs, and owner review beyond the checked no-claim native UI readiness-review template.

gate_axes.axis: Window input, IME, accessibility, and page-tree spike
gate_axes.required_evidence: Executable reference-platform workflow matrix, windowing, input, IME, accessibility, page-tree composition, clipboard, drag-drop, localization, zoom, high contrast, forced colors, reduced motion, crash recovery, renderer hang, GPU-loss, manual assistive-technology transcripts, supported screen-reader coverage, VoiceOver, Narrator, NVDA, Orca, UI-GATE-10, latency, resource traces, and owner review beyond the checked no-claim native UI readiness-review template.

adapter_axes.required_evidence: Versioned ShellSnapshot update ordering, stale-state rejection, trace hashes, and proof that toolkit state cannot become the durable source of truth.
Command epochs, cancellation, deadlines, confirmation, rejection reasons, service-side authority checks, and negative tests for stale command delivery.
View identity, document epoch, device generation, page-surface handle, fallback, synchronization, and ownership boundaries.
Native role, name, state, value, focus, event, page-tree composition, and screen-reader expectation mappings.
Trace, screenshot, accessibility snapshot, redaction, command rejection, adapter fault-injection, and crash or GPU-loss records.
Adapter trait boundaries, event-loop ownership, toolkit object lifetime, update delivery, replacement drill, and package proof.
Negative tests proving the toolkit cannot own navigation, profile, permission, credential, agent, Plug-in, persistence, or update authority.

framework_axes.required_evidence: Equivalent shell task evidence for Slint, Vizia, and Floem or GPUI or an owner-approved reduced scope with explicit reason and expiry.
Accepted ADR-0014 with toolkit selection, non-selection reasons, escape path, replacement cost, and residual risk.
Platform accessibility, IME, text input, keyboard navigation, focus, and shortcut behavior across supported reference platforms.
Evidence that the toolkit can host brokered page surfaces without owning page, renderer, profile, credential, or permission authority.
Crash, renderer hang, GPU-loss, device rebuild, and software fallback behavior under the same reference shell tasks.

framework_axes.axis: performance memory binary latency frame-pacing energy
framework_axes.required_evidence: Measured startup, memory, binary size, latency, frame-pacing, energy, and resource-attribution evidence on the selected reference platform.
Reviewed license, dependency, provenance, advisory, replacement, maintenance, and package/runtime-exclusion evidence.
Package proof that trusted chrome release builds contain no Electron, Tauri, system webview, Node, runtime React, runtime DOM, or runtime CSS parser.

page_surface_axes.required_evidence: Typed page-surface handles bind view ID, document epoch, device generation, resource owner, and stale-handle rejection.
Brokered surface handle proof prevents toolkit or renderer bypass of process, profile, origin, site, and frame boundaries.
Document and device generations reject stale frames, stale handles, wrong view IDs, and device-loss reuse.
Resize, scale, damage, color, alpha, deadline, presentation, release acknowledgement, and synchronization behavior are executed.
Input, IME, hit-test, focus, accessibility routing, and page-tree composition preserve profile, view, origin, and document identity.
Occlusion and capture policy deny unauthorized capture and keep trusted chrome and page content separation explicit.
Renderer crash, renderer hang, GPU-loss, device rebuild, stale-handle, and release-ack timeout failures are injected and recorded.
Deterministic software fallback behavior is implemented, bounded, measured, and tied to user-visible recovery behavior.
Latency traces, frame-pacing traces, accepted ADR-0016, UI-GATE-7 evidence, compositor ownership decision, and owner review exist.

fixture_axes.required_evidence: Semantic design-token registry produces rendered fixtures for typography, spacing, density, color, state, focus, motion, and iconography.
Adapter-specific browser chrome fixtures cover navigation, identity, permissions, profile, Space, and recovery states.
Tabs, Spaces, and command field fixtures cover normal, error, overflow, keyboard, focus, screen-reader, and localization states.
Permission prompts and agent confirmations preserve origin, document epoch, confirmation, denial, revoke, and stop controls.
Resource manager, settings, and recovery UI fixtures cover resource pressure, crash recovery, protected work, and user control.
Keyboard, focus, screen-reader roles, names, states, order, and live updates are captured in platform accessibility snapshots.
Forced-color, high contrast, reduced motion, contrast, and motion behavior are rendered and reviewed on supported platforms.
Density, localization, bidirectional text, text expansion, truncation, and error-state outputs are generated and reviewed.
Rendered component-fixture evidence is generated for the selected adapter and compared against toolkit-neutral contracts.

accessibility_axes.required_evidence: Executable reference-platform workflow matrix names platforms, toolkit adapter, page-surface stub, fixture set, retained artifacts, and owner review.
Windowing, input, pointer, keyboard, text entry, IME composition, cancellation, and recovery behavior are executed.
Accessibility tree snapshots and page-tree composition diffs prove chrome and page subtree identity, focus, origin, and document epoch handling.
Clipboard and drag-drop workflows preserve permission, profile, origin, file, text, and untrusted data boundaries.
Localization, zoom, high contrast, forced colors, and reduced motion workflows execute with retained screenshots and accessibility records.
Manual assistive-technology transcripts cover VoiceOver, Narrator, NVDA, and Orca with supported screen-reader coverage boundaries.
Crash recovery, renderer hang, GPU-loss, focus restoration, and user-work preservation are executed and reviewed.
Input, accessibility, event coalescing, and resource traces include latency, memory, CPU, and artifact-retention metadata.
UI-GATE-10 accessibility readiness evidence is accepted before any accessibility readiness or screen-reader coverage claim.

release_exclusion_axes.required_evidence: Trusted chrome package proof contains no Electron runtime, dependency, launcher, or bundled browser engine.
Trusted chrome package proof contains no Tauri runtime, WebView bridge, or release-path dependency.
Trusted chrome package proof contains no system webview, remote-rendering fallback, or OS web content control.
Trusted chrome package proof contains no Node runtime and no runtime JavaScript authority in release chrome.

release_exclusion_axes.axis: runtime React DOM CSS parser exclusion
release_exclusion_axes.required_evidence: Trusted chrome release builds contain no runtime React, runtime DOM, or runtime CSS parser.
Toolkit callbacks cannot own navigation, profile, permission, credential, agent, Plug-in, persistence, update, page-surface, or renderer authority.
Package, dependency, SBOM, advisory, license, provenance, and native notice evidence is owner reviewed.
Toolkit replacement drill, adapter escape path, migration cost, and rejected-candidate record are owner reviewed.

owner_review_axes.required_evidence: Named ui-runtime owner and accessibility independent reviewer accept or reject the review with current evidence.
Every current source record, validator output, artifact path, ADR, gate, and residual risk is collected before review.
UI adapter, framework bake-off, component fixture, page-surface, window/input/accessibility, and native UI readiness validators pass with retained output.
ADR-0013, ADR-0014, and ADR-0016 are accepted or explicitly held with owner-approved reason and expiry.
PB-003, PB-004, PB-005, PB-014, PB-015, UI-GATE-7, UI-GATE-10, and TASK-000006 are accepted, rejected, or held with evidence.
Every exception is owner-approved, time-bounded, risk-linked, support-boundary-linked, and reflected in readiness registries.
Documentation, readiness, task, crosswalk, risk, ADR, repository-map, and research-index records are updated after review.

rejection_rules.condition: This checked no-claim native UI readiness-review template is cited as completed readiness evidence.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject native UI readiness, toolkit selection, accessibility readiness, page-surface approval, and release-path UI approval.

rejection_rules.condition: Owner reviewer, independent reviewer, selected toolkit, adapter strategy, or reference platform is null, placeholder, title-only, self-approved, or unqualified.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject owner review, independent review, and selected-platform claims.

rejection_rules.condition: ADR-0013, toolkit-neutral contracts, native adapter prototype evidence, or no toolkit-owned authority negative tests are missing.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject adapter readiness and trusted-chrome architecture claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Equivalent framework adapter evidence, accepted ADR-0014, license, dependency, provenance, replacement, or package proof is missing.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject UI toolkit selection and framework bake-off success claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Page-surface protocol, typed handle, brokered surface handle, UI-GATE-7, ADR-0016, compositor ownership, or fault evidence is missing.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject page-surface approval and compositor ownership claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Rendered design-token, component-fixture, adapter-specific fixture, keyboard, focus, screen-reader, appearance, density, localization, or error-state evidence is missing.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject fixture completion, trusted-chrome readiness, and accessibility readiness claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Reference-platform workflows, page-tree proof, manual assistive-technology, VoiceOver, Narrator, NVDA, Orca, UI-GATE-10, IME, keyboard, or screen-reader evidence is missing.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject accessibility readiness and screen-reader coverage claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Release-path package proof does not exclude Electron, Tauri, system webview, Node, runtime React, runtime DOM, or runtime CSS parser.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject trusted-chrome and release-path UI approval claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Toolkit callbacks, state, or adapter objects can own navigation, profile, permission, credential, agent, Plug-in, persistence, update, page-surface, or renderer authority.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject native adapter prototype and trusted-chrome readiness claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Owner review or independent accessibility review is missing, stale, self-approved, or not tied to retained evidence.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject readiness promotion and TASK-000006 execution claims.

rejection_rules.condition: ADR-0013, ADR-0014, ADR-0016, UI-GATE-7, or UI-GATE-10 is cited without accepted owner-reviewed evidence.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject the corresponding architecture, page-surface, or accessibility claim.

rejection_rules.condition: Passing repository validation or a no-claim template is cited as proof of browser behavior, UI readiness, accessibility, performance, release, or production support.
A document removes unsupported behavior, residual risk, claim boundary, no-claim wording, or prohibited claim language without replacement evidence.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject the change and keep PB-003, PB-004, PB-005, PB-014, and PB-015 partial.
